MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-15-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on the following motions:

(1) Defendant SF Weekly's motion for summary judgment;
(3) Defendant SF Weekly's motion for terminating and monetary sanctions and

Dept. 220, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Cynthia Poliseri, #11449.

G. Whitney Leigh, Esq. appeared for plaintiff John Geluardi, who was not present at today's hearing.
Helen McFarland, Esq. appeared for defendants SF Weekly, et al.

After hearing argument by counsel, and good cause appearing, the Court rules as follows:

The Court shall issue a formal ruling on forwith on defendants' motion for summary, but the Court's tentative decision is to DENY the motion as to causes of action 1, 2, 3 & 5 of the second amended complaint.

In response to several of defendants' documents production requests, plaintiff contends that the documents do not exist, and therefore cannot be produced. Accordingly, no document in any of the catagories not responded to by plaintiff shall be introduced at trial. Plaintiff shall respond to special interrogatory requests 36 through 45. Additionally, plaintiff's response to special interrogatory number 34 is inappropriate and shall be expanded upon.

Defendants' request for terminating sanctions is DENIED. Defendants' request for monetary sanctions is GRANTED. Plaintiff shall pay defendant sanctions in the amount of $750.00 & $500.00, respectively. These amounts shall be paid to defendants' counsel within thirty (30) days of notice of entry of order. Defendants' request for sanctions for having to oppose plaintiff's discovery motion is DENIED.

With regards to plaintiff's request for production of documents, defendants shall produce documents in response to request numbers 19 & 20 with documents for the period of the last four years.

Counsel for defendants shall prepare the Order After Hearing.

Ntc Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-08-2011 Continued To Nov-15-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 220

Mtn To Compel Responses To Interoggs; To Compel Responses To Req For Production Of Docs
Off Calendar Nov-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on the following motions:

(1) Defendant SF Weekly's motion to compel responses to request for production of doucments;
(2) Defendant SF Weekly's motion for summary judgment;
(3) Defendant SF Weekly's motion for terminating and monetary sanctions and
(4) Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents and for sanctions.
Dept. 220, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Kris Case #13142.

G. Whitney Leigh, Esq. appeared for plaintiff John Geluardi, who was not present at today's hearing.
Helen McFarland, Esq. appeared for defendants SF Weekly, et al.

After hearing argument by counsel, and good cause appearing, the Court rules as follows:

(1) The parties reached an agreement in part on defendant's motion to compel further responses for production of documents. Plaintiff contends that in response to several of defendants' requests, the documents do not exist and therefore cannot be produced. Accordingly, plaintiff may not offer any of these documents in question at trial. Plaintiff shall respond to interrogatory requests 36 through 45. Additionally, plaintiff's response to special interrogatory number 34 is inappropriate and shall be expanded upon. Counsel shall meet and confer prior to the November 15th hearing to resolve this issue. Documents in response to defendants' requests shall be produced on or before the November 15th hearing.

(2) Defendant's motion for summary judgment is CONTINUED to November 15, 2011 @ 9:00 a.m. Parties waive notice.

(3) Defendant's motion for terminating and monetary sanctions is CONTINUED TO November 15, 2011 @ 9:00 a.m. and

(4) In response to plaintiff's request for production of documents, the parties shall meet and confer prior to the November 15th hearing to resolve all outstanding discovery issues, and all documents in response to plaintiff's discovery requests shall be produced on or before the November 15th hearing.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the Order After Hearing and submit to the Court for review.
